name: Indonesian Nasi Goreng
steps: Stir-fry the cold rice with the garlic and soy sauce.
Top with a fried egg.

ingredients_full.note: cooked and cooled, ideally a day ahead

steps_detailed: Mash the shrimp paste with the garlic, onion, and chili into a rough paste - traditionally pounded in a mortar, this is the aromatic base the whole dish is built on and shouldn't be skipped even in small quantity.
Fry the paste in a little oil until fragrant, 2-3 minutes.
Add the cold, day-old rice, breaking up clumps, and stir-fry over high heat 5-6 minutes.
Stir in the soy sauce and palm sugar.
Fry the egg separately, sunny-side up, and top the rice with it.

Nasi goreng's dark color and savory depth come specifically from shrimp paste and sweet soy - without them it's simply fried rice with vegetables, missing the ingredient that actually defines the dish across Indonesia.

name: Indonesian Bubur Ayam
steps: Simmer the rice and chicken with the turmeric in 2.5 cups water for 30-35 minutes.
Shred the chicken through.
steps_detailed: Simmer the rice with the chicken, garlic, and turmeric in 3 cups water over low heat, stirring occasionally, 35-40 minutes, until the rice breaks down into a thick, smooth porridge.
Shred the chicken back through and season with the soy sauce.
Top with the crisp-fried onion.

name: Indonesian Fried Rice Noodles (Bihun Goreng)
steps: Scramble the egg, then add the cooked noodles, bean sprouts, and soy sauce.
Stir-fry 3-4 minutes until well combined.
steps_detailed: Scramble the egg in the oil, then push aside.
Add the garlic, cooked noodles, bean sprouts, and soy sauce, and stir-fry 4-5 minutes over high heat until well combined.
A simple, common Indonesian breakfast noodle dish.

name: Indonesian Gado-Gado

ingredients.ingredient: Peanuts, all types, dry-roasted, with salt

steps: Arrange the cabbage, green beans, and sliced egg together.
Blend the crushed peanuts with a little water into a sauce and spoon over the top.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Peanuts, all types, dry-roasted, with salt

steps_detailed: Blanch the green beans until just tender, then combine with the shredded cabbage and sliced hard-boiled egg on a plate.
Blend the crushed peanuts with the fish sauce, lime juice, and a splash of warm water into a thick, spoonable sauce.
Spoon generously over the vegetables - gado-gado, meaning roughly 'mix-mix,' is a beloved Indonesian vegetable salad with peanut sauce.

name: Indonesian Soto Ayam
steps: Simmer the chicken with the turmeric in the broth until tender, 20-25 minutes, then shred.
Combine with the cooked noodles and broth.
steps_detailed: Simmer the chicken with the garlic and turmeric in the broth for 22-28 minutes until very tender, then shred and return to the pot.
Ladle over the cooked noodles, top with bean sprouts, and finish with a squeeze of lime - soto ayam, a turmeric-spiced chicken soup, is found across Indonesia in countless regional variations.

name: Indonesian Sate Ayam

ingredients.ingredient: Peanuts, all types, dry-roasted, with salt

steps: Marinate the chicken with the soy sauce and garlic for at least 20 minutes.
Thread onto skewers and grill until charred and cooked through, 10-12 minutes.
Blend the peanuts with a little water for a dipping sauce.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Peanuts, all types, dry-roasted, with salt

steps_detailed: Marinate the cubed chicken with the soy sauce and garlic for at least 30 minutes.
Thread onto skewers and grill over medium-high heat, turning occasionally, 12-15 minutes until well charred and cooked through.
Blend the crushed peanuts with the sugar and a splash of warm water into a thick dipping sauce.
Serve with the sauce and rice cakes if you have them - satay is beloved across Indonesia.

name: Indonesian Bakso
steps: Shape the ground beef into small meatballs.
Simmer the meatballs in the broth for 12-15 minutes until cooked through.
Serve over the noodles with bean sprouts.
steps_detailed: Mix the ground beef with the garlic and shape into small, firm meatballs.
Simmer the meatballs in the beef broth for 14-18 minutes until cooked through and firm.
Stir in the soy sauce.
Ladle the broth and meatballs over the cooked noodles, top with bean sprouts and cilantro - bakso, a beloved meatball soup, is sold on street carts throughout Indonesia.

name: Indonesian Rendang

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Brown the beef with the onion and turmeric.
Add the coconut milk and simmer, uncovered, 1.5-2 hours, stirring occasionally, until the sauce reduces and darkens.
Serve with rice.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the onion, garlic, ginger, and turmeric over medium-high heat, 10-12 minutes.
Add the coconut milk and toasted shredded coconut, bringing to a simmer.
Cook uncovered over low heat 1.5-2.5 hours, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has reduced dramatically, turned deep brown, and the oil has separated.
Serve with rice - rendang originates from West Sumatra's Minangkabau people and is treasured across Indonesia and beyond.

name: Indonesian Ayam Goreng
steps: Simmer the chicken with the garlic and turmeric in a little water for 15 minutes.
Drain and fry in the oil until deeply golden and crisp, 5-6 minutes per side.
Serve with rice and sambal.
steps_detailed: Simmer the chicken with the garlic, ginger, turmeric, and fish sauce in a cup of water for 15-18 minutes until nearly cooked through, then drain well and pat dry.
Fry the par-cooked chicken in the oil over medium heat, 6-8 minutes per side, until deeply golden and crisp outside.
Serve with rice and sambal - this double-cooking method, first simmered then fried, gives ayam goreng its distinctive tenderness inside a crisp crust.

name: Indonesian Grilled Fish (Ikan Bakar)
steps: Rub the fish with the turmeric and garlic.
Grill over medium-high heat, 4-6 minutes per side, until cooked through.
Finish with the lime juice.
steps_detailed: Combine the turmeric, garlic, jalapeno, and half the oil into a paste, and rub thoroughly over the fish.
Marinate 15-20 minutes, then grill over medium-high heat, brushing with the remaining oil, 5-7 minutes per side.
Finish with a generous squeeze of lime juice - grilled fish is central to Indonesia's coastal cooking.

name: Indonesian Semur (Sweet Soy Braised Beef)

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Brown the beef with the onion.
Add the soy sauce, brown sugar, and a splash of water, and simmer, covered, 1.5-2 hours until tender.
Serve with rice.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the onion and garlic over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the soy sauce, brown sugar, and beef broth.
Cover and simmer over low heat 1.5-2.5 hours until the beef is completely tender and the sauce has reduced to a dark, glossy consistency.
Serve with rice - semur's sweet, dark soy flavor reflects Indonesia's long history with Dutch and Chinese trade influences.

name: Indonesian Tempeh Goreng
steps: Marinate the tofu with the garlic and soy sauce for 10 minutes.
Fry in the oil until golden and crisp on all sides, about 3-4 minutes per side.
steps_detailed: Press the tofu briefly to remove excess moisture, then cube it (standing in for tempeh, Indonesia's beloved fermented soybean cake).
Marinate with the garlic, soy sauce, and turmeric for 10-15 minutes.
Fry in the oil over medium-high heat, 3-5 minutes per side, until deeply golden and crisp.
A simple, popular Indonesian snack and side dish.

name: Indonesian Pisang Goreng
steps: Mix the flour with a little water and the sugar into a smooth batter.
Dip the banana in the batter and fry until golden, about 2-3 minutes per side.
steps_detailed: Mix the flour, sugar, shredded coconut if using, and enough water to form a smooth, coatable batter.
Dip the banana slices in the batter, coating evenly, and fry in the oil over medium heat, 2-4 minutes per side, until golden and crisp.
A beloved Indonesian fried banana snack.

name: Indonesian Krupuk-Style Cassava Crackers

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Cassava Flour Grain Free Tortillas, Cassava Flour

steps: Mix the cassava flour with the garlic and enough water to form a very thin batter.
Fry thin spoonfuls until crisp and puffed, about 1-2 minutes per side.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Cassava Flour Grain Free Tortillas, Cassava Flour

steps_detailed: Mix the cassava flour with the garlic, a pinch of salt, and enough water to form a very thin, pourable batter.
Fry thin spoonfuls in the hot oil, 1-3 minutes per side, until crisp, puffed, and lightly golden.
Krupuk, in countless forms, is a beloved crunchy accompaniment to nearly every Indonesian meal.

name: Indonesian Martabak Telur (Savory Stuffed Pancake)
steps: Make a simple dough from the flour and water, and roll thin.
Brown the beef with the onion, then mix with the beaten egg.
Spoon the filling onto the dough, fold into a package, and fry until golden and crisp on both sides.
steps_detailed: Make a simple, elastic dough from the flour, a pinch of salt, and water, and roll it out very thin.
Brown the ground beef with the onion and soy sauce, then mix with the beaten egg off the heat.
Spoon the filling onto the center of the dough, fold in the sides to form a sealed package.
Fry in the oil over medium heat, 3-4 minutes per side, until golden and crisp on both sides.
Slice into wedges and serve hot - martabak telur is a beloved Indonesian street food, especially popular at night markets.

name: Indonesian Es Cendol

ingredients.note: cooked, as a cendol jelly stand-in

steps: Warm the coconut milk with the brown sugar until dissolved.
Combine with the cooked noodles.
Chill before serving.

ingredients_full.note: cooked, as a cendol jelly stand-in
cooked, as a red bean topping

steps_detailed: Warm the coconut milk with the brown sugar over low heat until fully dissolved, then chill.
Combine the chilled coconut mixture with the cooked noodles (standing in for the green rice-flour jelly strands) and cooked kidney beans.
Serve well chilled over crushed ice - es cendol is a beloved cold Indonesian dessert.

name: Indonesian Pisang Rai (Coconut Banana)
steps: Dip the plantain in the rice flour batter and simmer 5-6 minutes.
steps_detailed: Dip the plantain slices in a thin batter made from the rice flour and coconut milk.
Simmer in gently boiling water 5-6 minutes until the coating is cooked through and the plantain is tender.
Roll the warm pieces in the grated fresh coconut to coat.

name: Indonesian Klepon
steps: Wrap the dough around the palm sugar, seal well, and boil until they float, 4-5 minutes.
Roll in the coconut.

ingredients_full.note: ground into flour, or use rice flour

steps_detailed: Mix the glutinous rice flour with warm water into a soft, pliable dough.
Pinch off small pieces, flatten, and wrap each around a small piece of palm sugar, rolling into a smooth ball and sealing well so none of the sugar leaks out.
Boil in water 4-5 minutes until they float, then drain.
Roll the warm balls in the grated coconut.
Klepon's whole appeal is the burst of molten palm sugar when you bite into it - the wrap needs to be sealed completely, or the sugar leaks out into the boiling water instead of staying inside.

name: Indonesian Sambal Terasi
steps: Blend the jalapeno and tomatoes into a coarse paste.
Fry briefly in a little oil, then stir in the fish sauce and lime juice.
Serve as a fiery condiment.
steps_detailed: Blend the jalapeno, tomatoes, and garlic into a coarse paste.
Fry the paste in the oil over medium heat for 4-5 minutes until fragrant and slightly darkened.
Stir in the fish sauce and lime juice.
This nods to sambal terasi, which traditionally includes fermented shrimp paste - one of the most common sambals across Indonesia.

name: Indonesian Peanut Sauce (Bumbu Kacang)

ingredients.ingredient: Peanuts, all types, dry-roasted, with salt

steps: Simmer the crushed peanuts with the coconut milk and sugar for 5-6 minutes until thickened.
Stir in the lime juice off the heat.
Serve with satay, gado-gado, or grilled vegetables.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Peanuts, all types, dry-roasted, with salt

steps_detailed: Simmer the crushed peanuts with the coconut milk, sugar, fish sauce, and jalapeno if using, stirring frequently, for 6-8 minutes until thickened.
Stir in the lime juice off the heat.
The essential sauce behind satay and gado-gado, and endlessly versatile across Indonesian cooking.
